control_design.note: Single memory in collection; nearest-neighbor search of top_k=5 for old and new queries could not distinguish 'stored vector retained' from 'new vector correctly written but still nearest-of-one'.

limitations: no direct inspection of stored embedding
hit-set membership treated as evidence of stored-vector identity
conclusion_narrative: V08 concluded that Mem0 does not refresh the derived vector on update(text=...). Under review this conclusion was found to be an artifact of under-controlled experimental design. See record v08.1.scenario7.controlled_experiment for the corrected result and evidence. This record is retained as required by the R6 retraction protocol; it is not deleted.

alternative_explanations_ruled_out: 'new vector still nearest-of-one' — ruled out by having 5 unrelated control memories in the collection
'hybrid lexical scoring masking retained vector' — reduced by using maximally unrelated old/new target phrases and comparing stored embedding bytes directly
'ranking artifact from Chroma' — bypassed by fetching stored embedding via mem.vector_store.collection.get(ids=..., include=['embeddings']) before and after
control_design.note: Read-only inspection of the configured Chroma backend was used as the primary signal; retrieval-rank was corroborating only.

limitations: embedding-model HuggingFace snapshot revision is not pinned; a future revision could shift retrieval rank/score without invalidating the qualitative conclusion
evidence_source 'backend_inspection' is legitimate but is not a Mem0 public-API probe
conclusion_narrative: Under corrected experimental design (five distinctive control memories, maximally unrelated old/new phrases, direct fetch of stored embedding from the Chroma collection) Mem0 refreshes the derived vector on update(text=...). The stored embedding bytes changed with L2 delta ~ 1.374 across unit-norm vectors; retrieval-rank signal corroborates. This record supersedes v08.scenario7.probe and confirms Mem0 has NATIVE derived-vector-refresh behavior for this configuration.
